# Understanding Curve Stable Pools

Curve Pools are liquidity pools on the Curve Finance protocol designed for trading assets with similar values, such as stablecoins or ETH-based derivatives.

These pools allow users to deposit assets into a shared liquidity pool, where they facilitate trades between different tokens. In return, liquidity providers earn:

* trading fees generated by swaps within the pool
* incentives paid by protocols that desire deeper liquidity to operate and grow, such as lending derivative and yield bearing derivative protocols.&#x20;

Curve Pools are optimized to minimize slippage and maximize capital efficiency for assets that are expected to maintain similar prices. This makes them a foundational component of many yield-generating strategies within DeFi.\
\
Here is an example of a USD Curve Stable Pool: [The PmUSD-crvUSD SP on Ethereum](https://www.curve.finance/dex/ethereum/pools/0xecb0f0d68c19bdaadaebe24f6752a4db34e2c2cb/deposit). \
\
This is an example of an ETH Curve Stable Pool: [The msETH-WETH SP on Ethereum. ](https://www.curve.finance/dex/ethereum/pools/0xa4c567c662349bec3d0fb94c4e7f85ba95e208e4/deposit)
